questions: The idea text references 'solana_client.py' for items C7 (dynamic priority fees) and F16 (structured confirm logging). There is no file at src/features/payments/solana_client.py — the relevant file is src/features/grants/solana_client.py (the shared SolanaClient class used by both grants and payouts providers). The plan targets src/features/grants/solana_client.py. Is that the right target, or did you intend to create a new solana_client.py inside src/features/payments/?

A3 (consolidate _redact_wallet): should the shared helper live in src/common/discord_utils.py, or in a new src/common/redaction.py module? discord_utils.py has the right import graph but is Discord-specific; the redaction helper itself is chain-agnostic. My default is discord_utils.py (minimal churn) unless you prefer a new module.

A3: admin_chat/tools.py has a separate _redact_wallet_address helper with a different signature (accepts Any, not just Optional[str]) and additional _redact_wallet_row/_redact_payment_row wrappers. Should this pass also unify those, or leave them as a separate follow-up? My default is to leave them alone (they are distinct behavior).

F15 asks for the invariant checker to run 'daily via CI or cron'. This repo uses Railway — should I add a scripts/ cron entry, a GitHub Actions workflow, or just ship the script and leave wiring as an operator task? My default is to ship the script plus a README note in docs/runbook-payments.md and leave the scheduler wire-up as an info-priority follow-up.

D10 investigation: if the race between recover_inflight and the worker loop turns out to be real, my default fix is a conditional update that only reclaims processing/submitted rows older than 2 minutes. Is 2 minutes acceptable, or is there a tighter bound you want (e.g. matching worker tick interval)? If you want a different mechanism entirely (e.g., a generation counter on the row), tell me and I'll plan for that.

D11: the preferred fix for same-second admin collision is millisecond precision in producer_ref (Option A). Acceptable, or do you want full randomness (Option B with uuid suffix) to permanently break idempotency on this path?

B4: my heuristic for 'blockhash expired' in the reconcile_with_chain method is 'submitted_at older than 150 seconds'. Solana's blockhash lifetime is ~60–90 seconds; 150 is a safety margin. Is that margin acceptable, or do you want a real getLatestBlockhash-based check (more RPC calls but authoritative)?

success_criteria.criterion: All 14 polish items from the idea are either implemented or explicitly deferred with a written justification in the plan artifact.
A1: PaymentService.request_payment is decomposed into the 5 named helpers; public signature and return values unchanged; all existing tests pass without modification.
A2: PaymentService.__init__ raises ValueError when test_payment_amount is below 2_000_000 lamports; duplicate guard removed from main.py; new unit test covers this.
A3: _redact_wallet is defined exactly once in src/common/ and imported by payment_service.py, payment_cog.py (or post-cleanup worker cog), and db_handler.py.

B4: execute_retry_payment and execute_release_payment call reconcile_with_chain before mutating state; the four decision branches (reconciled_confirmed, reconciled_failed, allow_requeue, keep_in_hold) are each covered by a unit test.
B5: /payment-resolve slash command (admin-only) and admin_chat execute_payment_resolve tool both exist, call reconcile_with_chain, and append to tx_signature_history.
B6: Worker startup migrates legacy provider='solana' rows to solana_grants or solana_payouts; unknown producers go to manual_hold; unit test covers all three branches.
C7: SolanaClient.send_sol uses a dynamic priority fee (75th percentile of recent fees) clamped to [floor, ceiling] with fallback-on-error; unit test covers percentile math, clamps, and fallback.
C8: Admin DM failure falls back to ADMIN_FALLBACK_CHANNEL_ID; both-failed path logs at ERROR level; unit test covers Forbidden and double-failure paths.
C9: A distinct 'rpc_unreachable' manual_hold reason is emitted when the confirmation RPC is unreachable (as opposed to a legitimate confirmation timeout); unit test covers the distinction.
D10: tests/test_payment_race.py exists and passes; a written verdict ('proven safe' or 'fixed with commit N') is recorded as a file-level comment.
D11: A regression test reproduces or refutes the same-second admin collision; if reproduced, producer_ref precision is upgraded and the test becomes a regression guard.
D12: tests/test_payment_state_machine.py uses hypothesis RuleBasedStateMachine; hypothesis is added to requirements.txt; all five invariants are asserted.
docs/payments.md and docs/runbook-payments.md are committed with the specified sections.
scripts/check_payment_invariants.py exists, implements all five checks (a–e), exits nonzero on any violation, and has a fixture-based test for synthetic violations.
F16: SolanaClient.confirm_tx emits a structured log with event='tx_confirm_decision' on all three decision branches (confirmed, not_found, errored).
Full pytest suite passes after every phase lands.
No regression in existing grants or admin_chat payment flows (verified via tests/test_admin_payments.py and tests/test_solana_client.py).
Phase A refactor does not introduce any behavior change — same inputs produce same outputs and same log messages.
docs/payments.md readable by a new engineer in under 15 minutes (target ≤400 lines).
docs/runbook-payments.md covers every non-happy-path scenario listed in the idea.
scripts/check_payment_invariants.py runs successfully against prod DB (or a production-like snapshot) and reports no false positives on a healthy dataset.
After this pass, an operator can sleep at night with real money in the wallets — subjective, operator-reported confidence.
Daily CI or cron wire-up for check_payment_invariants.py is in place (deferred to operator as a follow-up per Question 4).

assumptions: The cleanup megaplan (payment-subsystem-follow-up-20260410-2339) will have landed before Phase A starts, producing PaymentWorkerCog + PaymentUICog split and the PaymentActor-based confirm_payment signature. If cleanup has not landed when this plan executes, Phase A is paused until it has.
The admin-DM approval megaplan will have landed before Phase D (specifically D12) runs, so the hypothesis state machine sees the final set of PaymentActorKind values including ADMIN_DM.

The idea's reference to 'solana_client.py' in items C7 and F16 means src/features/grants/solana_client.py (the shared SolanaClient), not a new file inside src/features/payments/. The SolanaClient is the thing that actually calls the RPC.
_redact_wallet consolidation lands in src/common/discord_utils.py (smallest graph change) unless the user requests a dedicated redaction module.

B4 blockhash-expiry heuristic uses a 150-second submitted_at age as a safe approximation of Solana's ~90s blockhash lifetime, avoiding an extra getLatestBlockhash RPC call per reconcile. If the user wants an authoritative check, swap in Question 7's alternative.
D11 fix defaults to millisecond-precision producer_ref (Option A) rather than a random UUID suffix (Option B); cheapest fix and preserves idempotency for legitimate retries.

scripts/check_payment_invariants.py is shipped as a standalone script; daily scheduling wire-up (cron, GitHub Actions, Railway scheduled job) is deferred to the operator as an info-priority follow-up.
Property-based tests use hypothesis's RuleBasedStateMachine against a fake DB handler, not a live Supabase instance — so the test suite remains hermetic and fast.
admin_chat/tools.py's separate _redact_wallet_address helper is out of scope for the A3 consolidation because it has a different signature and wrapper semantics.

F16's structured log uses Python's standard logging 'extra' dict; downstream log aggregation (if any) will pick up the event key without further wiring — if the bot's log handler strips 'extra', a follow-up is needed but is out of scope here.

D10's fix-if-gremlin-found is a conditional update restricting recover_inflight to rows with updated_at older than 2 minutes, not a generation counter or schema change. If a more invasive fix is required, it becomes a follow-up.
